codeReview.summary: Implementation covers the feature goal: random relay test-port selection was replaced with OS-assigned ephemeral allocation (port 0), tests were updated to use the actual bound port from server.port after start, and restart/offline cases now discover then reuse a concrete port. loadRelayConfig was updated to permit port 0, and no random test-port formula remains in the test tree.

issues.description: A shared getTestPort helper was added but the modified tests still keep local getTestPort definitions instead of importing this helper, leaving duplication and reducing the value of the shared utility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared-state docs do not clarify whether test utility functions should be centralized vs. per-file, which likely contributed to adding an unused shared helper while retaining duplicated local helpers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: test/helpers/test-port.ts:16 defines shared getTestPort(); test/relay/async-core.test.ts:36 and test/auth/relay-guards.test.ts:21 still define local getTestPort(); searching for "test-port" imports in /test returns no matches.

summary: Reviewed commit d7ccf228cd37f230593bb1e7ee7f556fbe5d19e3 for realtime-watch-fix-test-port-collision-allocation. The fix removes random-port collision risk by using OS-assigned ports and post-start bound-port capture across affected integration tests, and I found no blocking defects.
